title: Ballimon maritimum
intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant Ballimon maritimum. However, I found that you might be referring to Ballota nigra or Ballota pseudodictamnus, but more likely it might be Ballota maritima.

Ballota maritima, also known as Basal or Sea Horehound, is a perennial plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ballota maritima include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ballota maritima include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat coughs, colds, and digestive issues.
